name: Tadalafil did not change exercise-induced ischemia time in CAD but lowered blood pressure and reinforced nitrate risk
statement: In stable CAD subjects, tadalafil 10 mg did not significantly alter time to exercise treadmill-induced ischemia versus placebo, but lowered systolic blood pressure at rest, during exercise, and after exercise, and potentiated nitroglycerin-related hypotension.
population_or_context: Stable CAD adults age 53 to 75 years undergoing treadmill testing.
time_horizon: Acute crossover treadmill and nitroglycerin challenge.
quantitative_note: Time to ischemia was 13 min 31 s with tadalafil versus 13 min 36 s with placebo; SBP was lower at rest (-7 mmHg), during ETT (-10 mmHg), and after ETT (-13 mmHg); more subjects had SBP <85 mmHg after nitroglycerin with tadalafil.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports time-to-ischemia, SBP effects, nitroglycerin hypotension, and nitrate contraindication.

caveats: Nitrate interaction is a hard boundary.
population_profile: Stable CAD adults older than target; safety-relevant for exercise and occult cardiovascular disease.
indication_context: CAD exercise safety and drug-interaction physiology.
endpoint_measure: Exercise treadmill time to ischemia, systolic blood pressure, nitroglycerin hemodynamic response.
effect_estimate: No ischemia-time benefit; blood pressure reduced and nitrate hypotension risk increased.
safety_findings: Concomitant nitrates and tadalafil are contraindicated due hypotension risk.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy performance, high for safety boundary in exertion plus cardiovascular disease/nitrate context.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small CAD crossover study and no healthy performance outcome.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil safety evidence; not an exercise-benefit claim.
